heading: 1.1 Blog post or tech note?
text:
rOpenSci blog features two types posts: long form blog posts broad readership, shorter tech notes aimed technical audience. word “post” refers generically either type.
provide details selected examples blog posts tech notes .
Tech notes written narrower audience wants details.
may include information software release major new features, breaking changes, significant new documentation.
provide something reader glean documentation .Blog posts usually published weekly Tuesdays.
Tech notes can published weekday.
given promotional treatment rOpenSci.

text:
Since posts contributed community members packages passed software peer review, use type post example outline components consider including post.
components included post peer reviewed package.

feel like can’t resist sharing (small corner ) world?create package?

deep technical knowledge software development.
domain scientists interested use package developed.
might reading way consider can contribute open source project.

heading: 2.2.1 Why?
text:

click -line post takes reader author page links online home, possibly Twitter GitHub profile(s), list content ’ve authored site.
staff leadership team members, editors software peer review, members Code Conduct Committee, rOpenSci title also listed author page.

heading: 2.2.2 How?
text:
don’t already author page, create folder called yourfirstname-yourlastname local copy roweb3/content/author/.
can accents, middle initials, hyphens appear name name folder appropriately. can tricky examples .folder, create file called _index.md information online presence.
can copy template (get via roblog installing via install.packages("roblog", repos = "https://dev.ropensci.org") ). author-file-template.md  minimum, provide name link Twitter, GitHub, GitLab username.
Add usernames ID’s without “@” “https:…”. link field can personal website URL, example.
code:
---
name: Author Name
link: website URL or other online presence
twitter: Twitter username
github: GitHub username
gitlab: GitLab username
keybase: Keybase ID
orcid: ORCID ID
---

heading: 2.3.1 New Post Addin
text: blogdown New Post RStudio addin creates post draft correct location fills post YAML based metadata ’ll entered.1Install whoami (install.packages("whoami")), blogdown version 1.1.10 (moment writing, available GitHub, remotes::install_github("rstudio/blogdown")).Install Hugo (preview post): blogdown::install_hugo().Re-start R.RStudio, open forked roweb3 project.Create new post running Addins > New Post blogdown:::new_post_addin().Leave “Categories” blank.Enter title, need worry title case stage.Enter name whoami wasn’t able guess .Choose correct date.Enter new slug default one long.Choose “blog” Subdirectory drop-menu.Choose Archetype, Rmd md, drop-menu.Also choose correct Format: .Rmd Rmd, Markdown (.md) md. Never choose .RMarkdown.Ignore Categories.Select “tech notes” tag tech noteSelect relevant tags /create new onesClick “Done”, post draft created opened.

heading: 2.4.1 YAML
text: YAML sets metadata post. YAML post template, comments explain components:
code:
---
slug: "post-template"
title: Post Title in Title Case
# Delete the package_version line below if your post is not about a package
package_version: 0.1.0
author:
  - Author Name1
  - Author Name2
# Set the date below to the publication date of your post
date: 2020-03-10
# Minimal tags for a post about a community-contributed package 
# that has passed software peer review are listed below
# Consult the Technical Guidelines for information on choosing tags
tags:
  - Software Peer Review
  - packages
  - R
  - community
# The summary below will be used by e.g. Twitter cards
description: "A very short summary of your post (~ 100 characters)"
# If you have no preferred image for Twitter cards,
# delete the twitterImg and twitterAlt lines below 
# - Replace "blog" with "technotes" as needed
# - Note "/" between year/month/day
twitterImg: blog/2019/06/04/post-template/name-of-image.png
twitterAlt: "Alternative description of the image"
# the text below is for populating the "share on Twitter" button
# if deleted, the title of the post will be used
tweet: "A post about blabla by @username!"
---

heading: 2.4.1.1 Subject tags
text: Add tags YAML post make findable. Browse page lists tags use re-use existing tag rather creating new one e.g. ‘packages’ exists, use , rather ‘package’.post peer-reviewed package, use ‘Software Peer Review’, ‘community’, ‘packages’, package name, others see fit.

heading: 2.4.5 Citations
text: add citations, refer body post:list sources bottom post:
code:
Citation of the primary literature[^1].
Citation of an R package[^2].
Citation of a website[^3].[^1]: Sciaini, M., Fritsch, M., Scherer, C., & Simpkins, C. E. (2018). NLMR and landscapetools: An integrated environment for simulating and modifying neutral landscape models in R. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 9(11), 2240-2248. <https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.13076>
[^2]: Elin Waring, Michael Quinn, Amelia McNamara, Eduardo Arino de la Rubia, Hao Zhu and Shannon Ellis (2019). skimr: Compact and Flexible Summaries of Data. R package version 2.0.2. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=skimr
[^3]: Hugo static site generator. https://gohugo.io/

text: get citation R package, run citation("packagename").get citation article, can use RStudio Addin rcrossref, get citation paper’s DOI running e.g.get citation article Google Scholar, find article, click quote symbol (search results article) open “Cite” window, copy APA style text.
code:
rcrossref::cr_cn("10.1111/2041-210X.13076", format="text", style="apa")

[1] "Sciaini, M., Fritsch, M., Scherer, C., & Simpkins, C. E. (2018). NLMRandlandscapetools: An integrated environment for simulating and modifying neutral landscape models inR. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 9(11), 2240–2248. doi:10.1111/2041-210x.13076"

heading: 2.5 Style Guide
text: formatting package names, functions, code, follow tidyverse style guidance. Format package names regular text (quotes).use markdown titles (can use headings).Use ## ### format headings post, .e. section titles ## (h2) subsections ### (h3), #### (h4).Use title case title post; use sentence case headings inside post.‘rOpenSci’ ‘ROpenSci’adding rOpenSci links post, use relative instead absolute URLs e.g. /blog/ instead https://ropensci.org/blog/.linking rOpenSci packages use docs.ropensci.org URL, e.g. https://docs.ropensci.org/drake/.Use Hugo shortcodes (html) add images, tweets, gists etc.R Markdown wrap Hugo shortcodes html preserve tags shown template.Instead using html widgets (DT, leaflet, etc.), include screenshot use link option Hugo figure shortcode direct readers online version widget.Use informative alternative text images.Add new line end sentence (makes diffs easier interpret easier editor suggest specific changes).

heading: 2.6.3 Author Checklist
text:
Pick appropriate checklist post ensure checked everything .
Hover mouse list copy-paste button appear 
top-right corner shaded area.

heading: 2.6.3.1 Posts on peer-reviewed packages
code:
* [ ] I have read the Content Guidelines.
* [ ] I have read the Technical Guidelines.
* [ ] I used or followed the R Markdown or Markdown template.
* [ ] I have followed the Style Guide.
* [ ] I created or updated my author metadata with correct folder name.
* [ ] I have added relevant tags after browsing existing tags (incuding "community" tag).
* [ ] I have added the "tech notes" tag if this is a technote.
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_lint_md()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_check_urls()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md.
* [ ] I have added the tags - Software Peer Review, my-packagename.
* [ ] I have added the package-version YAML tag.
* [ ] I have added acknowledgement of the reviewers' work (with links to reviewers).
* [ ] I have added a link to the software peer review thread.
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md.

heading: 2.6.3.2 Other posts
code:

* [ ] I have read the Technical Guidelines.
* [ ] I used or followed the R Markdown or Markdown template.
* [ ] I have followed the Style Guide.
* [ ] I created or updated my author metadata with correct folder name.
* [ ] I have added relevant tags after browsing existing tags (incuding "community" tag).
* [ ] I have added the "tech notes" tag if this is a technote.
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_lint_md()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_check_urls()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md.

heading: 5.1.2 Editor checklist - Posts on peer-reviewed packages
code:
* [ ] post follows Content Guidelines
* [ ] post follows Style Guide
* [ ] title is in Title Case
* [ ] publication date is ok
* [ ] alternative text of images is informative
* [ ] Twitter metadata looks ok (paste post preview link in [Twitter card validator](https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator); might have to click twice on "Preview card")
* [ ] author metadata is provided with correct folder name
* [ ] html not included in pull request of Rmd post
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_lint_md()` on index.md
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_check_urls()` on index.md
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md
* [ ] YAML subject tags are ok ("tech notes" for tech notes; "community" for non-staff non-editor)
* [ ] YAML package-version included
* [ ] YAML subject tags - software peer review, packagename
* [ ] acknowledges and links to reviewers
* [ ] links to peer review thread

heading: 5.1.3 Editor checklist - Other posts
code:
* [ ] post follows Content Guidelines
* [ ] post follows Style Guide
* [ ] title is in Title Case
* [ ] publication date is ok
* [ ] alternative text of images is informative
* [ ] Twitter metadata looks ok (paste post preview link in [Twitter card validator](https://cards-dev.twitter.com/validator); might have to click twice on "Preview card")
* [ ] author metadata is provided with correct folder name
* [ ] html not included in pull request of Rmd post
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_lint_md()` on index.md
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_check_urls()` on index.md
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md
* [ ] YAML subject tags are ok ("tech notes" for tech notes; "community" for non-staff non-editor)

text: first tweet thread tweet must includethe post title,link post,tag author’s account, IRL name,tweet must provide alternative description included image.tweet can include link software peer review thread.tweet can includehashtags,additional account names,caveats next subsections.

Tag account(s) post authors package authors reviewers.already included author metadata, can search account tag ifit active,anonymous,least partly used professionally owner.doubt, use person’s name, time permitting, contact person whose account ’d like tag.package wrapping service present Twitter can tag account (e.g. tweet rredlist accesses IUCN Red List might tag IUCN Twitter account).tagging accounts include sentence e.g. “Thanks @account1”/ “told @account2”.

heading: 5.3.4.1 Selecting relevant hashtags
text:
tweet post related package R thing, use #rstats hashtag.tweet package post, make package name hashtag, since rocitations Twitter account announce package citations.Check last tweets using hashtag except #rstats hashtag see whether used expect.

heading: 5.3.4.2 Adding hashtags
text: use many hashtags tweet make account look like spam account / greedy attention.Post hashtags bottom tweet, make rest content tweet easier read.Capitalize letters word hashtags including several words .e. use lower upper camelCase (e.g. #RLadies #rladies).posting thread, use hashtag pollute timeline hashtag.

using emojis, use many .

text: tweets, gifs optional.One can include gif created post (e.g. animated plot).generally Giphy gif since might interpreted differently people know /less context gif question (e.g. TV show).using gif add description typing [Gif alt: descriptive phrase] bottom tweet.tweeting twitter.com, Feb 2020, compose image descriptions setting turned accessibility settings, can add alt descriptions gifs images.

heading: 5.3.7 Example 2-tweet thread:
code:
[blog] “rmangal: making ecological networks easily accessible” 

New post by @KCazelles & @SVissault in our Software Peer Review series 

🔗 https://ropensci.org/blog/2019/10/21/rmangal/

1/2 
#rstats #rmangal #SoftwarePeerReviewShoutouts 🙏 in the post to reviewers Anna Willoughby & @thomasp85, rmangal contributors https://docs.ropensci.org/rmangal/authors.html, & all ecologists who have spent countless hours collecting data

🔎Read the rmangal open software peer review thread: https://github.com/ropensci/software-review/issues/332

2/2

heading: 6.1 GitHub team
text: rOpenSci blog editors member @ropensci/blog-editors team.team maintained @stefaniebutland moment.Blog editors can tagged ropensci repo, particular roweb3, writing @ropensci/blog-editors (e.g. :wave: @ropensci/blog-editors, trouble adding citations post!).Blog editors can tagged ropensci repo, particular roweb3, writing @ropensci/blog-editors (e.g. :wave: @ropensci/blog-editors, trouble adding citations post!).Blog editors write access roweb3 repo.Blog editors write access roweb3 repo.Blog editors “code owners” roweb3/content/blog, roweb3/content/commcalls according roweb3’s CODEOWNERS file PR touching file paths automatically request review. one posts review, marked “behalf @ropensci/blog-editors”.Blog editors “code owners” roweb3/content/blog, roweb3/content/commcalls according roweb3’s CODEOWNERS file PR touching file paths automatically request review. one posts review, marked “behalf @ropensci/blog-editors”.adding new blog editor, team maintainer ropensci adminensures user enabled 2FA,ensures user enabled 2FA,adds team Member (default role).adds team Member (default role).blog editors can filter notifications, e.g. using reason:team-mention filter.blog editors don’t necessarily write access blog-guidance repo encouraged make PRs.

heading: 6.2 Posts authored by staff
text: Staff members can bypass review tech notes can choose request review blog editors.Staff members’ blog posts scheduled together Stefanie Butland.Staff members responsible creating discussion forum entry post must tweet rOpenSci Twitter account (directly via Tweetdeck rOpenSci team) tweeting personal account.

heading: A Template - Post (md)
text: Use template described Start post template Walkthrough code snippets.Markdown template saved /content/blog/YYYY-MM-DD-slug/index.mdYou can hover top-right corner template make copy-paste button appear.
code:
---
slug: "post-template"
title: Post Title in Title Case
# Delete the package_version line below if your post is not about a package
package_version: 0.1.0
author:
  - Author Name1
  - Author Name2
# Set the date below to the publication date of your post
date: 2020-03-10
# Minimal tags for a post about a community-contributed package 
# that has passed software peer review are listed below
# Consult the Technical Guidelines for information on choosing tags
tags:
  - Software Peer Review
  - packages
  - R
  - community
# The summary below will be used by e.g. Twitter cards
description: "A very short summary of your post (~ 100 characters)"
# If you have no preferred image for Twitter cards,
# delete the twitterImg and twitterAlt lines below 
# - Replace "blog" with "technotes" as needed
# - Note "/" between year/month/day
twitterImg: blog/2019/06/04/post-template/name-of-image.png
twitterAlt: "Alternative description of the image"
# the text below is for populating the "share on Twitter" button
# if deleted, the title of the post will be used
tweet: "A post about blabla by @username!"
---

This is the Markdown (.md) template for a blog post or tech note. 
To generate your post with R Markdown (.Rmd), use that template instead.

Throughout this template, including the YAML, 
you should change "post-template" to the slug of your post, 
and "2019-06-04" to your publication date.

Save this file under /content/blog/YYYY-MM-DD-slug/index.md in the local copy of your roweb3 fork.

## Section heading in sentence case

Citation of the primary literature[^1]. 

Citation of a website[^2]. 

Citation of an R package[^3].

### Subsection heading

We recommend the use of [Hugo shortcodes](https://gohugo.io/content-management/shortcodes/) to include images, tweets, videos, gists, etc.

**Add an image** by using a Hugo shortcode. The image is saved under `/content/blog/YYYY-MM-DD-slug/name-of-image.png`.

{{< figure src = "name-of-image.png" width = "400" alt = "this is the alternative text" >}}

Consult the Technical Guidelines for tips on changing image size, alignment, and for advice on alternative text.

**Embed a tweet** by using a Hugo shortcode. 

{{< tweet 1138216112808529920 >}}


**Add citation or footnote** text by using the format below 

[^1]: Sciaini, M., Fritsch, M., Scherer, C., & Simpkins, C. E. (2018). NLMR and landscapetools: An integrated environment for simulating and modifying neutral landscape models in R. Methods in Ecology and Evolution, 9(11), 2240-2248. <https://doi.org/10.1111/2041-210X.13076>
[^2]: Elin Waring, Michael Quinn, Amelia McNamara, Eduardo Arino de la Rubia, Hao Zhu and Shannon Ellis (2019). skimr: Compact and Flexible Summaries of Data. R package version 1.0.7. https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=skimr
[^3]: Hugo static site generator. https://gohugo.io/

chapter: D Author Checklist - Posts on peer-reviewed packages
heading: D Author Checklist - Posts on peer-reviewed packages
text:
Use template described Pre-submission checks.Copy checklist first comment pull request.
can hover top-right corner template make copy-paste button appear.
code:
* [ ] I have read the Content Guidelines.
* [ ] I have read the Technical Guidelines.
* [ ] I used or followed the R Markdown or Markdown template.
* [ ] I have followed the Style Guide.
* [ ] I created or updated my author metadata with correct folder name.
* [ ] I have added relevant tags after browsing existing tags (incuding "community" tag).
* [ ] I have added the "tech notes" tag if this is a technote.
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_lint_md()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran `roblog::ro_check_urls()` on index.md (optional).
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md.
* [ ] I have added the tags - Software Peer Review, my-packagename.
* [ ] I have added the package-version YAML tag.
* [ ] I have added acknowledgement of the reviewers' work (with links to reviewers).
* [ ] I have added a link to the software peer review thread.
* [ ] I ran a spell-check on index.md.
